mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals an easing monetary stance that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, regardless of the current level.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following declining FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more relative to its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-like trigger), it signals an emerging labor market downturn that predicts equity risk-off pressure and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger fires, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL determines the real policy rate; when FEDFUNDS growth outpaces CPIAUCSL inflation (rising real rates), financial conditions tighten and equities underperform over the next 20 trading days, an effect not captured by either series alone.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY change) versus forward 20d returns is not more negative than the IC of FEDFUNDS alone over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In an expansionary macro regime (GDPC1 rising and UNRATE below 5%), a rising HOUST signals confident consumer demand and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; in a contractionary regime the same rise in HOUST fails to predict positive returns because housing gains reflect distressed low-rate refinancing rather than genuine demand.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% and UNRATE < 5%
regime_hint: expansionary growth regime with low unemployment
failure_condition: During periods satisfying the expansionary trigger, HOUST increases fail to precede positive 60d forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the conditional effect disappears when compared against the unconditional HOUST-return relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent trading week as market participants incrementally price in stronger near-term earnings.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing INDPRO momentum and forward 5d returns remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month annualized growth rate of PAYEMS decelerates below 1%, it signals weakening labor demand that historically precedes reduced consumer spending and corporate revenue downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159626 thousand as of asof=t-3mo
failure_condition: When the deceleration trigger is active, forward 20d returns are positive in more than 55% of instances or show no significant difference from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with a stable or falling UNRATE jointly indicate robust consumer demand supported by healthy employment, reinforcing each other to produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone carries the same predictive strength because RSAFS gains during rising unemployment often reflect credit-financed spending rather than durable demand.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-UNRATE signal's Rank IC with forward 20d returns does not exceed the maximum of the individual single-series ICs by at least 0.02 over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where GDPC1 growth has been positive for multiple consecutive quarters, further acceleration in GDPC1 signals overheating risk and increased probability of policy tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as markets price in reduced accommodation; in early or mid-cycle regimes the same GDPC1 acceleration is associated with positive returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth accelerates above prior quarter for 3+ consecutive quarters
failure_condition: During periods satisfying the late-cycle acceleration trigger, 60d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, indicating the overheating mechanism does not hold.
